_meta.note: Backlog clear: (1) Stacy Garrity (PA) evidence-based state-rubric refinement (replaces the establishment_r archetype with cited positions; reset_unspecified). (2) Amy Klobuchar (MN) dual-office conversion to the 2026 MN governor's race (matches the existing Blackburn/Bennet 'sitting U.S. Senator running for governor' pattern — office names the gov race + 'sitting U.S. Senator', level state, scored on her federal Senate record). Blackburn (TN) + Bennet (CO) were already correct (verified current; primaries Aug 6 / Jun 30 not yet held) and are untouched. All web-verified 2026-06-02.

profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-02 — Evidence-based state-rubric refinement (replaces the establishment_r archetype). Sitting PA Treasurer + 2026 R gubernatorial nominee. Documented conservative across life, school choice, fiscal discipline, voter ID, ICE cooperation, and guns (GOA-endorsed); declined to affirm marriage when directly asked (scored a non-affirmation). Honest nulls where no clear public position was found (religious liberty, public justice, refuse-federal-overreach, ESG).

stacy-garrity-2026.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE evidence refinement, state tier] Replaced the establishment_r archetype with cited evidence. Conservative record: sanctity of life (self-described '100 percent pro-life,' celebrated Dobbs, 'Pennsylvania needs a PRO-LIFE governor,' called for PA abortion restrictions — with some general-election softening); family/child sovereignty (school choice — charters, cyber, private, scholarships, vouchers); economic stewardship (fiscal-conservative Treasurer, opposes raiding the rainy-day fund, tax cuts); election integrity (strong voter ID + accurate rolls + anti-noncitizen-voting); border (ICE cooperation); self-defense (Gun Owners of America-endorsed; backs constitutional/permitless carry). KEY DEVIATION: when directly asked about marriage equality she declined to affirm — 'I don't really want to talk about hypotheticals' — scored as a documented non-affirmation of man-woman marriage. Mixed/null: ESG (signed the 2021 GOP-treasurers anti-ESG letter yet runs a Treasury ESG framework -> null); religious liberty, public justice, refuse-federal-overreach left null (no clear public position). Archetype 68 -> evidence 88.

1.note: Self-described '100 percent pro-life' (position 'never changed'); celebrated Dobbs; 'Pennsylvania needs a PRO-LIFE governor' (2022); called for action to restrict PA abortion access (some general-election softening noted).

Directly asked about marriage equality, declined to affirm — 'I don't really want to talk about hypotheticals.' Evasion on a core issue -> does not affirm man-woman marriage (rubric editorial rule: silence on a big issue when asked).

0.note: Backs school choice — charters, cyber charters, private schools, scholarships, vouchers; 'parents, not bureaucrats, should be deciding the best way to educate their kids.'

2.note: Fiscal-conservative Treasurer; opposes using the rainy-day fund to cover budget gaps; backs income/property/inheritance-tax cuts.

4.note: Mixed ESG record — signed the 2021 GOP-treasurers letter against Biden's anti-fossil-fuel pressure, yet developed a Treasury ESG investment framework. No clear divest-from-ESG-funds action -> null (not credited, not penalized).

1.note: Supports strong voter ID, accurate voter rolls, and cracking down on noncitizen voting / administrative loopholes.
Pro-enforcement; pledged to cooperate with ICE / the Trump administration on immigration.
Endorsed by Gun Owners of America (no-compromise 2A group) — HARD evidence of opposition to red-flag/AWB/mag-limit/registry restrictions.

2.note: Strong Second Amendment supporter; backs concealed/permitless ('constitutional') carry (HB659).

amy-klobuchar.notes_set: U.S. Senator (MN, DFL), sitting since 2007; re-elected 2024 (Senate seat not up till 2030). [2026-06 RESOLUTE dual-office update, state-race listing] Running for Governor of Minnesota in 2026 — announced Jan 29, 2026 after Gov. Tim Walz dropped his re-election bid; won the DFL state-convention endorsement May 29, 2026 (fought off a challenge from the left); primary Aug 11, 2026, general Nov 3. One-record dual-office: the active race is the governor's race; she keeps her Senate seat unless she wins. Scored on her federal Senate record (progressive_d archetype) pending an evidence-based refinement. Web-verified 2026-06-02.
